item.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
item.description: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

item.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
item.description: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

item.title: Judge orders release of more than $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse, defamation case
item.description: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

item.title: Sneak peek: My Mother's Murder Trials
item.description: ENCORE: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports in a two-part "48 Hours" airing Saturday, July 11 from 9-11 p.m. ET on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Trump says he's not sure he wants to make a deal with Iran
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday said he wasn't sure he wants to make a deal with Iran and threatened more strikes against the country. CBS News' Nancy Cordes reports and Robert B. Murrett, professor of practice at Syracuse University, joins with analysis.

item.title: Boomer Parents: It's Time to Cough it Up | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
Americans have been anxiously awaiting the so-called "great wealth transfer," where trillions of dollars are expected to pass down from Baby Boomers to their kids. But people are living longer and holding onto their assets. Jill says it's time to start opening up the purse strings now. Plus, caller questions: Toby asks whether sterling silver jewelry is actually a worthwhile investment, and Dave wants to know if taking a pay cut to improve his quality of life is financially realistic.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

item.title: Trump hails "unity" at NATO summit, despite recent tensions
item.description: President Trump concluded the NATO summit with a press conference touting the "tremendous love" exhibited by the allies.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire over, "going to hit them hard again tonight"
item.description: Trump said he's not sure he wants to make a deal with the Iranians, but also said he doesn't think the war will "start again."

item.title: El-Sayed, Stevens clash over Israel and campaign money in Michigan Democratic Senate debate
item.description: Progressive former health director Abdul El-Sayed and centrist Rep. Haley Stevens debated as the two vie for the lead in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary. Nikole Killion has the latest.

item.title: Democrats call for Platner to exit Maine Senate race as new allegations emerge
item.description: Despite growing calls for Senate hopeful Graham Platner to step aside, the Maine Democrat has only denied recent abuse allegations against him. High-profile Democrats are now urging Platner to exit the race as a key election deadline looms. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Tyler Robinson faces Day 3 of preliminary hearings in Charlie Kirk murder trial
item.description: Tyler Robinson, the suspect in Charlie Kirk's murder, is due back in court today as he faces Day 3 of preliminary hearings. This comes after prosecutors unveiled footage of him on the Utah college campus where Kirk was killed. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Trump says dealing with Iran is "a waste of time" and warns of U.S. strikes
item.description: President Trump warned of future strikes on Iran, saying the U.S. is "going to hit them hard again tonight." CBS News' Weija Jiang, Charlie D'Agata and Ramy Inocencio report on Iran, and contributor Sam Vinograd comments on Ukraine's attempts to press NATO allies for more support.

item.title: Argentina defeats Egypt in World Cup while Switzerland stuns Colombia in penalty kicks
item.description: Argentina defeated Egypt 3-2 in their Round of 16 match, while Switzerland stunned Colombia 4-3 in penalty kicks to advance to the quarterfinals in the World Cup.

item.title: First major 2026 Atlantic hurricane forecast predicts below-average season
item.description: Colorado State University has updated its annual Atlantic hurricane forecast, predicting nine named storms and four hurricanes may develop during the 2026 season.

item.title: Can you qualify for debt forgiveness if you're still making minimum payments?
item.description: Making the minimum every month feels responsible, but is it disqualifying you from the relief you actually need?

item.title: Trump reacts to rising gas prices after he announces Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: President Trump addressed the war with Iran and the country's nuclear weapons program while taking questions from reporters on Wednesday at the NATO summit in Turkey. Mr. Trump claimed Iran's nuclear material is so far underground that no country besides the U.S. would be able to harvest it, and also spoke about trade relationships and rising prices in America.

item.title: Nolan Wells' family seeks answers after missing Mississippi teen found dead
item.description: The family of Nolan Wells, who was found dead after a July 4 boat trip to Horn Island, has hired civil rights attorney Ben Crump.

item.title: Oil prices leap, stocks drop after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: President Trump called negotiations with Iran "a waste of time" and said the ceasefire with Iran is over, shocking the stock market and spiking gas prices in reaction to the renewed unrest in the Middle East. Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will grant Ukraine's request to license Patriot defense system
item.description: "A little birdie told me this, about the fact that we'll give them the right to make Patriots," President Trump told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

item.title: Telegram used to share rape videos and drugging tips, prosecutors say
item.description: The suspects posted videos of their attacks and referred to women as "cars," sedatives as "fuel" and rape as "driving," according to court documents.

item.title: Trump says U.S. is probably going to "hit Iran hard again tonight" after saying ceasefire is over
item.description: President Trump spoke at the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, where he said the U.S. will possibly "hit Iran hard again tonight" following overnight strikes and an announcement that the ceasefire is over. CBS News' Weijia Jiang, Courtney Kealy and contributor Samantha Vinograd have more.

item.title: Missing teen Nolan Wells found dead near Mississippi island
item.description: The body of missing teen Nolan Wells was discovered off the coast of Horn Island, Mississippi, officials said. Wells was last seen with a group of friends days before.

item.title: "Ted Lasso" actor Cristo Fernández shares new children's book "Fútbol Is Life"
item.description: "Ted Lasso" actor Cristo Fernández shares the inspiration behind his new children's book "Fútbol is Life!" The bilingual book draws on Fernández's childhood experiences growing up in Guadalajara and is "a love letter to the game" of soccer.

item.title: What's behind the surge in mahjong's popularity in the U.S.?
item.description: Mahjong, a traditional Chinese tile game, is rapidly increasing in popularity across the U.S. Caitlin Huey-Burns headed to a mahjong parlor to see why the game is seeing renewed appreciation.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Kentucky governor requests update on Mitch McConnell's health
item.description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear formally requested an update from Sen. Mitch McConnell amid a weekslong hospitalization that has prompted growing speculation about his health.

item.title: Toddler declared dead in pool accident found alive in hospital morgue
item.description: Two police officers saw possible signs of life, but the child was still taken to the hospital's "cold room" after being treated by staff, according to police documents.

item.title: USPS raising the price of a Forever stamp starting on Sunday
item.description: The cost of a first-class Forever stamp has climbed 41% since 2021, and postal officials have signaled they want prices to rise even more.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.
